title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After hearing a muffled sigh, Anthony climbs through a locked window into a dim room at the Hirstmill property. Inside, he discovers a pale, emaciated foreigner with a bandaged head whose hands and feet are tied. As Anthony approaches the prisoner, Hiram Fisk appears at the door holding a large blue automatic pistol. Fisk commands Anthony to raise his hands, noting that they had both been on the same train at Victoria Station.

Meanwhile, on Tuesday evening, the Earl of Carringham, Virginia, and Bunder gather in the study to discuss Anthony’s sudden departure. The Earl expresses unease regarding the lack of security and the disappearance of Fischer, who was last seen in the rose garden. Tredwell interrupts the discussion to announce the arrival of the French detective, Monsieur Lemont.

Lemont informs the group that he has discovered a suspicious guest who arrived from a remote location via South Africa. Virginia challenges Lemont, arguing that Victor von Winkle (Mr. Fuchs) is more suspicious than the missing Anthony Kede due to his secretive behavior near the murder scene. Lemont counters by revealing that Mr. Fuchs is actually a private detective for Pinkerton who came to the house to follow Victor Wang. As the discussion turns to the pursuit of their target, Virginia defends Anthony Kede, swearing he did not kill Prince Michael. Lemont suggests an alternative possibility: that Boris the Hessian killed the Prince out of revenge for past wrongs.
